$comment: Release self-declaration consumed by the unified registry's auto-core-release resolver (sh2-plugin-registry/scripts/resolve-core-candidate.mjs) at the git tag being released. The supports.* semver ranges decide whether a tagged core can be auto-matched with the latest published counterparts into a signed release candidate PR. Keep pluginApiVersion in sync with config/services.yaml (CI enforces it) and widen/bump supports.frontend deliberately - while the platform is pre-1.0, every 0.x minor is breaking. Raise supports.frontend's lower bound whenever core changes/removes an API contract the frontend depends on. Floor 0.1.30 is the first frontend that BOTH (a) adopts the field-naming unification - migration Version20260622165615 drops the `shared_` prefix from 47 style props (`shared_color`->`color`, `shared_variant`->`variant`, `shared_radius`->`radius`, ...; reserved `shared_height`/`shared_width`/`shared_icon` keep their prefix), so this core emits the unprefixed names a 0.1.23-0.1.28 frontend does not read - AND (b) handles the core 0.1.18 anonymous-access hardening: an anonymous `preview=true` is now rejected with 401, so frontend 0.1.30 gates SSR preview on a live session cookie and clears `sh_preview` on logout/expiry (mobile 0.1.10 ships the matching previewPolicy gate). A 0.1.29 frontend left a stale admin preview cookie 401-looping the public site against this core, so the floor moved 0.1.29 -> 0.1.30. The 0.1.30 floor still carries everything the previous floors required - the full 0.1.15 style-schema contract (mobile-rendering taxonomy: style `renderTarget`, required field `scope`, the unprefixed-common/web_/mobile_ field split with `pages.id_platform` removed in favour of the page access type) PLUS the alert/badge/avatar/button/login authoring-polish wave - so an older frontend can never be auto-assembled with this core into a release. The resolver refuses any incompatible frontend+backend pair in either direction. Core 0.1.20 adds the CMS-driven mobile-preview install/update endpoints (/cms-api/v1/admin/system/update/mobile-preview/*) and the `mobile_preview_version` field on /admin/system/version; both are ADDITIVE and optional, so supports.frontend stays >=0.1.30 (the core does not require the preview-update UI - frontend 0.1.32 adopts it and raises ITS supports.core floor to 0.1.20). Core 0.1.21 adds the `admin.mobile_preview.view` permission (migration Version20260623193630) gating the frontend's full-screen Live Preview surface; it is ADDITIVE (no new route, no schema change - the live preview reuses the existing mint+exchange routes), so supports.frontend stays >=0.1.30 (frontend 0.1.33 adopts the live preview and raises ITS supports.core floor to 0.1.21).
